User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the ServiceWalla platform.

Worker.title: Worker
Worker.description: Represents a service worker on the ServiceWalla platform.

reviewCount.description: The total number of reviews the worker has received.

averageRating.description: The worker's average rating, from 1 to 5.

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Represents a service offered on the ServiceWalla platform.

Booking.title: Booking
Booking.description: Represents a booking of a service by a user with a worker.

status.description: Status of the booking (e.g., pending, confirmed, completed, cancelled).

completionCode.description: A 4-digit code generated upon booking confirmation, provided by the customer to the worker to mark the job as complete.

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Represents a review of a service worker by a user.

workerId.description: The ID of the worker being reviewed.

userId.description: The ID of the user who wrote the review.

userName.description: The name of the user who wrote the review.

userPhotoURL.description: The photo URL of the user who wrote the review.

rating.description: The star rating given by the user, from 1 to 5.

comment.description: The text content of the review.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the review was submitted.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Only the user with the matching userId can read/write their profile data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores worker profiles. Only the worker with the matching workerId can read/write their profile data. The serviceIds array stores references to the worker's services.

params.name: workerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the worker.

definition.description: Stores service definitions. Open read access (for browsing). Write access is restricted (e.g., to admins). The workerIds array stores references to the workers providing the service.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the service.

definition.description: Stores bookings made by a specific user.  Authorization: The user with the matching `userId` can read/write bookings under their ID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: bookingId
params.description: The unique identifier of the booking.

definition.description: Stores bookings associated with a specific worker.  Authorization: The worker with the matching `workerId` can read bookings under their ID.

params.name: workerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the worker.

params.name: bookingId
params.description: The unique identifier of the booking.

definition.description: Stores reviews for a specific worker. Openly readable by all users, but only the user who created the review can write to it.

params.name: workerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the worker.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The unique identifier of the review.

definition.description: Stores frequently asked questions. Open read access. Write access restricted to admins (role-based, implemented via existence check in `/roles_admin/{uid}`).

params.name: faqId
params.description: The unique identifier of the FAQ.

definition.description: Documents the UIDs which have admin roles. Authorization: Only accessible by backend functions.

params.name: uid
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the ServiceWalla application, focusing on clear separation of concerns and Authorization Independence.  The structure leverages path-based ownership for user and worker data, enabling secure and efficient data access.  

**Authorization Independence:** This design avoids `get()` calls in security rules by denormalizing authorization data where needed.  Specifically, the `Booking` collection includes both `userId` and `workerId`, allowing security rules to validate access based on the authenticated user and the involved worker without needing to fetch parent documents. This ensures atomic operations for booking creation and modification.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structure supports secure list operations by segregating data based on ownership and role.  User and worker profiles are stored in dedicated collections with path-based ownership, enabling secure listing of user-owned or worker-owned resources. The FAQs collection is globally accessible for reading, fulfilling the requirement for listing FAQ items without complex filtering rules.

**Structure Overview:**
*   `/users/{userId}`: Stores user profiles.  Authorization: Only the user with the matching `userId` can read/write their profile data.
*   `/workers/{workerId}`: Stores worker profiles. Authorization: Only the worker with the matching `workerId` can read/write their profile data. The `serviceIds` array stores references to the worker's services.
*   `/services/{serviceId}`: Stores service definitions.  Authorization: Open read access (for browsing). Write access is restricted (e.g., to admins).  The `workerIds` array stores references to the workers providing the service.
*   `/users/{userId}/bookings/{bookingId}`: Stores bookings made by a specific user.  Authorization: The user with the matching `userId` can read/write bookings under their ID.
*   `/workers/{workerId}/bookings/{bookingId}`: Stores bookings associated with a specific worker. Authorization: The worker with the matching `workerId` can read bookings associated with them.
*   `/workers/{workerId}/reviews/{reviewId}`: Stores reviews for a specific worker. Authorization: Publicly readable, write access limited to the user who created it.
* `/faqs/{faqId}`: Stores frequently asked questions. Authorization: Open read access.  Write access restricted to admins (role-based, implemented via existence check in `/roles_admin/{uid}`).
* `/roles_admin/{uid}`: Documents the UIDs which have admin roles. Authorization: Only accessible by backend functions.
